lesson.title: Data Protection (POPIA)
lesson.explanation: Protection of Personal Information Act (2013, enforced 2021). 8 conditions: accountability, processing limitation, purpose specification, further processing limitation, information quality, openness, security safeguards, data subject participation. Your rights: access your data, correct it, object to processing, complain to Information Regulator (012 406 4818). Organisations must: appoint an Information Officer, register with Regulator, get consent for processing, notify breaches within 72 hours. Penalties: up to R10 million fine or 10 years imprisonment. POPIA applies to all organisations processing personal info in SA.

practice_problems.question: What is the first step when beginning work in Data Protection (POPIA)?
Describe the key regulation or standard that governs Data Protection (POPIA) in South Africa.
practice_problems.answer: See lesson content for specific SA regulations.
hints: Think about SANS standards or Acts
practice_problems.explanation: SA has specific standards for each trade.

misconceptions.misconception: You don't need formal training for Data Protection (POPIA)
misconceptions.correction: Proper training and certification are required for safety and legal compliance in SA.
misconceptions.why_students_think_this: People see informal work and assume no qualifications are needed.
